description:
Normalize all eyebrow/label/meta patterns to canonical 'text-sm font-medium tracking-widest uppercase' (delivered via .text-eyebrow / .text-label / .text-meta utilities). Eliminate the drift: ~12x text-xs vs ~8x text-sm, weights ranging across font-semibold, font-thin, unlabeled.

Per-site judgment: which sites are eyebrows (above a title), which are labels (forms/UI), which are meta (timestamps/credits/attribution). Spec captures the semantic distinction — visual rendering is identical for now.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: Eyebrow is the most-drifted pattern in the audit. Three sizes and three weights for what should be one chrome voice. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation.

Acceptance: All small uppercase mono labels go through .text-eyebrow, .text-label, or .text-meta. No remaining manual 'text-xs tracking-widest uppercase font-semibold' soup in src/. Plan file records per-site semantic assignments.
title: Eyebrow + label + meta normalization — adopt chrome utilities
